input.background: The ANOVA in Exercises 13.3 found that birth weight of four groups of infants differed significantly according to the smoking status of mothers (P = 0.042). Table 13.3 lists the data for the analysis. Data are also available online in the file SMOK_BW.SAV.
input.question: Conduct post hoc comparisons using the LSD method. Summarize these results in concise narrative form.

output.answer:
(a) Least squared difference (LSD) tests

| Null hypothesis          | x̄_i - x̄_j | SE      | P-value (2-sided) |
|--------------------------|------------|---------|-------------------|
| H₀: μ₁ - μ₂ = 0         | 0.0857     | 0.67848 | 0.901             |
| H₀: μ₁ - μ₃ = 0         | 1.1191     | 0.64690 | 0.098             |
| H₀: μ₁ - μ₄ = 0         | 1.6129     | 0.60512 | 0.014             |
| H₀: μ₂ - μ₃ = 0         | 1.0334     | 0.67848 | 0.143             |
| H₀: μ₂ - μ₄ = 0         | 1.5272     | 0.63876 | 0.026             |
| H₀: μ₃ - μ₄ = 0         | 0.4938     | 0.60512 | 0.424             |

Calculated by SPSS (Rel. 11.0.1. 2001. Chicago: SPSS Inc.)

Summary in concise narrative form: Differences between group 1 and group 4 and between group 2 and group 4 are statistically significant. The difference between group 1 and group 3 is marginally significant.

meta_info.caption: Smoking and birth weight, post hoc comparisons.
